title: Union type
body:
ESI will have need (potentially soon) for a union type. (For preserving and exposing type information over multiplexed wires, among other things.)

I have two questions:

1. Should this go in the `rtl` dialect along with `array` and `struct`? Or the `sv` dialect? (I think the `rtl` dialect.)
2. Should it be discriminated (tagged) or a C-style union? Should we have both?

I'm torn on number 2. If it's in the `sv` dialect, the semantics are governed by the SV spec. If, however, it's going to be in the `rtl` dialect I could see arguments either way. I think a discriminated union makes sense so as not to lose type information -- SystemVerilog natively supports discriminated (tagged) unions.

Any thoughts?
